criteria: The agent has read Section 3.1 completely to understand the Label Smoothing decomposition, including the regularization term and the error amplification term as formalized in Corollary 3.4 (Equation 5).
The agent has read Section 3.2 completely to understand the Max Suppression Regularization (MaxSup) formulation, including Definition 3.5 and the logit-level formulation in Equation 8.
The plan includes implementing the standard Cross-Entropy (CE) loss as the base loss function.
The plan includes implementing Label Smoothing (LS) loss using the logit-level formulation: L_LS = alpha * (z_gt - (1/K) * sum(z_k)), where z_gt is the ground-truth logit and z_k are all logits.
The plan includes implementing the MaxSup loss using the logit-level formulation: L_MaxSup = alpha * (z_max - (1/K) * sum(z_k)), where z_max is the maximum (top-1) logit.
The plan includes a linearly increasing alpha schedule that raises alpha from 0.1 to 0.2 over the course of training, applied to both LS and MaxSup.

The plan outlines experiments on ImageNet-1K using ResNet-18, ResNet-50, ResNet-101, and MobileNetV2 with the correct training configuration (SGD, momentum 0.9, weight decay 1e-4, batch size 2048, initial lr 0.85, cosine schedule, 200 epochs).
The plan outlines experiments using DeiT-Small on ImageNet-1K without CutMix or Mixup and without knowledge distillation, using the official DeiT codebase.
The plan outlines CIFAR-100 experiments with ResNet variants using initial lr 0.1 (reduced fivefold at epochs 60, 120, 160), 200 epochs, batch size 128, weight decay 5e-4.
The MaxSup loss function is implemented to compute z_max as the maximum logit over all K classes and the mean logit (1/K)*sum(z_k), then compute L_MaxSup = alpha * (z_max - mean_z).
The MaxSup loss function correctly identifies the top-1 logit dynamically for each sample regardless of whether the prediction is correct or incorrect (i.e., z_max = argmax(q) in logit space).
The total training loss is implemented as: L = H(y, q) + alpha * L_MaxSup = CE_loss + alpha * (z_max - mean_z), matching Algorithm 1 (step 9).
The Label Smoothing (LS) baseline is implemented using the logit-level formulation L_LS = alpha * (z_gt - (1/K)*sum(z_k)), consistent with Equation 4.
The linearly increasing alpha schedule is implemented: alpha(t) = 0.1 + 0.1 * (t/T), where t is the current epoch and T is the total number of epochs.
The gradient of L_MaxSup with respect to each logit z_k is correctly computed: alpha*(1 - 1/K) for z_max and -alpha/K for all other logits (Equation 9).
The Zipf-LS and OLS baselines are implemented using the authors' official codebases and hyperparameters for fair comparison.
The Logit Penalty baseline is implemented, which minimizes the l2-norm of the entire logit vector.

ResNet-18, ResNet-50, ResNet-101, and MobileNetV2 models are loaded/implemented for ImageNet-1K training with the specified optimizer (SGD, momentum=0.9, weight_decay=1e-4, batch_size=2048, lr=0.85, cosine schedule, 200 epochs).
DeiT-Small is implemented for ImageNet-1K training without CutMix or Mixup (disabled) and without knowledge distillation, using the official DeiT codebase.
ResNet variants are implemented for CIFAR-100 with lr=0.1 reduced fivefold at epochs 60, 120, and 160, batch size 128, weight decay 5e-4, 200 epochs.

The ablation study on LS components is implemented: separate 'regularization only' variant penalizing (alpha/M)*sum(z_gt - z_m for z_m < z_gt) and 'error amplification only' variants as described in Table 1, using DeiT-Small on ImageNet-1K without CutMix/Mixup.
The intra-class variation metric d_within (mean pairwise cosine distance within each class in penultimate-layer feature space) is implemented for ResNet-50 on ImageNet-1K.
The inter-class separability metric R^2 (coefficient of determination from class-mean-based linear regression in feature space) is implemented for ResNet-50 on ImageNet-1K.
The linear-probe transfer evaluation pipeline is implemented: freeze ResNet-50 features pretrained on ImageNet-1K, then fit a linear classifier on CIFAR-10 and report accuracy.
The fine-grained classification experiments on CUB-200-2011 and Stanford Cars datasets are implemented with the comparison methods (Baseline, LS, OLS, Zipf-LS, MaxSup).
The long-tailed CIFAR-10 classification experiments are implemented using ResNet-32 with Focal Loss, Focal Loss+LS, and Focal Loss+MaxSup at imbalance ratios 50 and 100.
The OOD/corrupted image classification experiment on CIFAR-10-C is implemented using ResNet-50, computing Error, NLL, and ECE metrics.

The semantic segmentation experiment is implemented: DeiT-Small pretrained on ImageNet-1K (with MaxSup or LS) is used as backbone for UperNet, fine-tuned on ADE20K, and evaluated with multi-scale mIoU.

The Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) visualization pipeline is implemented for DeiT-Small under MaxSup, LS, and baseline CE training, producing heatmaps highlighting class-discriminative regions.

The main ImageNet-1K classification experiments (Table 4) with ResNet-18, ResNet-50, ResNet-101, MobileNetV2 comparing Baseline, LS, Zipf-LS, OLS, MaxSup, and Logit Penalty are executed successfully without errors.
The DeiT-Small ImageNet-1K classification experiments (Table 5) comparing Baseline, LS, Zipf-LS, OLS, and MaxSup are executed successfully without errors.
The ablation study experiments on LS components (Table 1) using DeiT-Small without CutMix/Mixup are executed successfully.
The feature quality experiments measuring d_within and R^2 on ResNet-50/ImageNet-1K (Table 2) and the linear-probe transfer on CIFAR-10 (Table 3) are executed successfully.
The semantic segmentation experiment on ADE20K with UperNet+DeiT-Small (Table 9) is executed successfully.

The ablation on LS components (Table 1) shows: Baseline ~74.21%, LS ~75.91%, Regularization-only ~75.98%, Error-amplification-only ~73.63-73.69%, MaxSup ~76.12%, validating that MaxSup outperforms LS by removing the error amplification term.
MaxSup achieves the highest top-1 accuracy among all methods on ImageNet-1K for ConvNets: ResNet-18 ~69.96%, ResNet-50 ~77.69%, ResNet-101 ~78.18%, MobileNetV2 ~72.08% (Table 4).
MaxSup achieves the highest top-1 accuracy on CIFAR-100 for ConvNets: ResNet-18 ~77.82%, ResNet-50 ~79.15%, ResNet-101 ~79.41%, MobileNetV2 ~69.88% (Table 4).
DeiT-Small results (Table 5) show MaxSup achieves ~76.49% (+2.10%) top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K, outperforming LS (~76.08%, +1.69%), OLS (~76.16%, +1.77%), and Zipf-LS (~75.89%, +1.50%).

Feature quality results (Table 2) show MaxSup has the highest d_within (intra-class variation) among label-smoothing-based methods: ~0.293 (train), ~0.300 (val), indicating less intra-class compression than LS (~0.263/~0.254).
Linear-probe transfer results (Table 3) show MaxSup preserves near-baseline transfer accuracy on CIFAR-10 (~0.810), whereas LS degrades it to ~0.746 and Logit Penalty to ~0.724.
Fine-grained classification results (Table 6) show MaxSup achieves best performance on CUB-200-2011 (~82.53%) and Stanford Cars (~92.25%), surpassing LS, OLS, and Zipf-LS.
Long-tailed CIFAR-10 results (Table 7) show that Focal Loss+MaxSup outperforms Focal Loss+LS in overall accuracy across imbalance ratios (e.g., at ratio 50, test: MaxSup 81.4% vs LS 80.5%).
Semantic segmentation results (Table 9) show MaxSup-pretrained DeiT-Small achieves mIoU of ~42.8% on ADE20K, outperforming LS (~42.4%) and Baseline (~42.1%) with UperNet.

The alpha scheduler experiment (Table 11) shows that the linearly increasing schedule improves both LS (75.91% -> 76.16%) and MaxSup (76.12% -> 76.58%), with a greater gain for MaxSup (+0.46% vs +0.25%).
The extended linear transferability results (Table 12) show MaxSup consistently outperforms LS and OLS across CIFAR10, CIFAR100, CUB, Flowers, Foods, and Pets datasets in linear-probe accuracy.
